zh.Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可格亞特 亞爾姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Page於大約1629年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特時，她的父親Robert Page26歲，母親Lucia Warde26歲。她於1651年與William Moulton I在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可咸頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她在美國新罕布夏州住了大約17年。她去世於1699年7月13日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆咸頓享年71歲，埋葬在美國新罕布夏州洛金哈姆咸頓潘恩 葛洛夫 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Page于大约1629年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特时，她的父亲Robert Page26岁，母亲Lucia Warde26岁。她于1651年与William Moulton I在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可咸頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她在美国新罕布什尔州住了大约17年。她去世于1699年7月13日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆咸頓享年71岁，埋葬在美国新罕布什尔州洛金哈姆咸頓潘恩 葛洛夫 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Paget
meanings: French: diminutive of   Page  .
English: variant of   Padgett  .

variants.name: Paige
meanings: English (mainly Devon): variant of   Page  .

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.
French: diminutive of   Page  .

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.
Oldest Grave seen in Memorials List

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Page was born about 1629, in Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Page, was 26 and her mother, Lucia Warde, was 26. She married William Moulton I in 1651, in Hampton, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She lived in New Hampshire, United States for about 17 years. She died on 13 July 1699, in Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 71, and was buried in Pine Grove Cemetery, Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Page est née environ 1629, à Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Page, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lucia Warde, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé William Moulton I en 1651, à Hampton, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle a vécu à New Hampshire, États-Unis pendant environ 17 ans. Elle est décédée le 13 juillet 1699, à Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Pine Grove Cemetery, Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Page è nata il circa 1629 nel Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert Page, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Lucia Warde, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato William Moulton I nel 1651, nel Hampton, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Vissuto a New Hampshire, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 17 anni. ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Pageが約1629年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクオルメスビー サイント マーガレットで生まれた時，父Robert Pageは26歳，母Lucia Wardeは26歳であった。1651年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーノーフォルクハンプトンでWilliam Moulton Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女はアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州に約17年間住んでいた。彼女は1699年7月13日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムハンプトンで71歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ロクインガムハンプトンピン グローブ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Hampton, Rockingham, Colony of New Hamps: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 함프턴

ko.Hampton, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 함프턴
ko.Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 세인트 마거릿
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Page(이)가 대략 1629년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 세인트 마거릿에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Page(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucia Warde(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1651년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크 함프턴에서 William Moulton I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 뉴햄프셔 주에서 약 17년 동안 살았다. 그녀는 1699년 7월 13일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 함프턴에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 록잉함 함프턴 핀 그로브 세멧리에 묻혔다.

ru.Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, England: ормесби сеинт маргарет, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Page родилась около 1629 г. в местности ормесби сеинт маргарет, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert Page было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucia Warde, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Moulton I в 1651 г. в местности хамптон, норфолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она жила в местности Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты около 17 лет. Она умерла 13 июля 1699 г. в местности хамптон, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности паин гров кеметири, хамптон, роккингхам, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Page nació aproximadamente 1629, en Ormesby Saint Margaret, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Page, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lucia Warde, tenía 26 años. Se casó con William Moulton I en 1651, en Hampton, Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Vivió en Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos durante unos 17 años. Murió el 13 de julio de 1699, en Hampton, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultada en Pine Grove Cemetery, Hampton, Rockingham, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos.
